title: Greek Yogurt Chicken Wrap Recipe (430 Cal, 40g Protein) | Eati
introduction: Greek Yogurt Chicken Wrap is a high-protein meal delivering 40g of protein and 430 calories per serving — designed for muscle building and fat loss. With 10g of fat and 42g of carbohydrates, it fits comfortably into any macro-tracked eating plan. Log it in Eati to track your macros automatically and stay on target.

sections.heading: Ingredients
sections.content:
- 160g cooked chicken breast, shredded or sliced
- 1 large whole-wheat wrap (about 70g)
- 100g nonfat Greek yogurt
- 50g cucumber, diced
- 50g cherry tomatoes, halved
- 20g red onion, diced
- Fresh mint or dill
- Salt, pepper, lemon juice
- Optional: a few olives or feta

sections.heading: Instructions
sections.content:
1. Mix Greek yogurt with salt, pepper, lemon juice, and mint as a sauce.
2. Warm wrap in microwave 15 seconds to make it pliable.
3. Spread Greek yogurt sauce across the wrap.
4. Layer chicken, cucumber, tomatoes, and red onion.
5. Roll tightly. Slice in half.
6. Log in Eati: 'chicken wrap whole wheat 70g, Greek yogurt 100g, chicken 160g'.

sections.heading: Calories, Protein, Fat & Carbs
sections.content:
Per serving (estimated):

Calories: 430 kcal
Protein: 40g
Fat: 10g
Carbohydrates: 42g

Macro split: 37% protein / 21% fat / 39% carbs

Protein per 100 calories: 9.3g

Note: values are estimates based on typical ingredient weights. Log exact portions in Eati for precise tracking — the app calculates macros automatically from your description or a barcode scan.

sections.heading: Why Greek Yogurt Chicken Wrap Works for Muscle Gain and Weight Loss
sections.content:
Replacing mayo or sour cream with Greek yogurt in a wrap cuts fat in half while adding 10g of extra protein — a swap that simultaneously improves the macro profile and adds probiotic benefit.

Macro breakdown (estimated):
• 430 calories
• 40g protein
• 10g fat
• 42g carbohydrates

Wraps are underrated for muscle gain:
A whole-wheat wrap provides 25–30g of complex carbohydrates — enough to restore some glycogen post-workout without pushing total carbs too high. Paired with 40g of protein, this is a portable post-gym meal that requires zero cooking if chicken is prepped.

sections.heading: Variations and Serving Ideas
sections.content:
1. Low-carb (330 cal, 40g protein): use a low-carb tortilla (net 4g carb).
2. Mediterranean (450 cal, 40g protein): add hummus, roasted peppers, feta.
3. Spicy (440 cal, 40g protein): add sriracha or jalapeño.

sections.heading: Meal Prep, Storage & Time-Saving Tips
sections.content: Pre-cook chicken (4-day shelf life). Pre-mix yogurt sauce (3-day shelf life). Assemble wraps 2 days ahead wrapped in foil — they hold well refrigerated.

midArticleCta: Track the exact macros for Greek Yogurt Chicken Wrap in Eati — type what you ate or snap a photo and let the app calculate protein, calories, fat, and carbs instantly.
conclusion: Greek Yogurt Chicken Wrap delivers 40g of protein in 430 calories — a macro profile that makes it one of the most effective dishes for body recomposition. Add it to your weekly rotation, track each serving in Eati, and let consistent protein intake do the work.
metaTitle: Greek Yogurt Chicken Wrap Recipe: 430 Cal, 40g Protein, 10g Fat, 42g Carbs

faqs.question: How many calories in a Greek yogurt chicken wrap?
faqs.answer: About 430 calories — 40g protein, 10g fat, 42g carbs — using a 70g whole-wheat wrap and 160g chicken.

faqs.question: Can I use Greek yogurt instead of mayo in a wrap?
faqs.answer: Yes — nonfat Greek yogurt has 10× less fat and 4× more protein than mayonnaise. The texture is creamier than you'd expect and pairs perfectly with fresh vegetables and herbs.
